LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RICHARD ZITRIN, PAUL FRASETTO, AND ZITRIN & FRASETTO, LLP'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE COMPLAINT AS A MERITLESS SLAPP (C.C.P. SECTION 425.16). RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE DEMONSTRATED THAT THE CONDUCT AT ISSUE IS PROTECTED ACTIVITY UNDER THE FIRST PRONG OF CCP 425.16 (E)(1) AND (2) AS A WRITTEN STATEMENT MADE IN A JUDICIAL PROCEEDING IN A MATTER UNDER CONSIDERATION. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT ESTABLISHES A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING. THE CONDUCT AT ISSUE IS PROTECTED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE, CIVIL CODE 47(B). SILBERG V. ANDERSON (1990) 50 CAL. 3D 205, 212. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER ON MOTION.) THE COURT RULED ON BOTH DEFENDANTS EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO 1)DECLARATION OF DAVID REPLOGLE AND 2)SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF DAVID REPLOGLE, BOTH ORDERS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Ntc And Special Mtn To Strike The Complaint As A Meritless Slapp
Off Calendar Oct-06-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS RICHARD ZITRIN, PAUL FRASETTO, AND ZITRIN & FRASETTO, LLP'S Special Motion To Strike The Complaint As A Meritless SLAPP (C.C.P. Section 425.16).
APPEARANCES: David Replogle, Esq. of Law Offices of David Replogle 362-4700 for pltf & opposing party; Mark Goldowitz, Esq. of California Anti-SLAPP Project 510/486-9123 x301 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - granted. Defendants have demonstrated that the conduct at issue is protected activity under the first prong of CCP 425.16 (e)(1) and (2) as a written statement made in a judicial proceeding in a matter under consideration. Plaintiff has failed to present admissible evidence that establishes a probability of prevailing. The conduct at issue is protected by the litigation privilege, Civil Code 47(b). Silberg v. Anderson (1990) 50 Cal. 3d 205, 212. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER on motion.) Court ruled on both defendants evidentiary objections to 1)declaration of David Replogle and 2)supplemental declaration of David Replogle, both orders signed in open court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
